title: perf(core): use deque for result reassembly in RunnableRetry batch
body_text:
## Problem

`RunnableRetry._batch()` and `_abatch()` use `list.pop(0)` to reassemble batch outputs by merging a `results_map` (already-succeeded inputs) with remaining results consumed front-to-back.

`list.pop(0)` is **O(n)** per call because CPython must shift every remaining element. For large batch sizes this accumulates to **O(n²)** total work in the reassembly loop.

## Solution

Convert the `result` list to a `collections.deque` just before the reassembly loop and replace `.pop(0)` with `.popleft()`, which is **O(1)** amortised.

The original `result` list is not used after reassembly, so the conversion is safe. The deque is consumed exactly once, front-to-back.

## Testing

All 4 retry-related tests pass:
- `test_retrying`
- `test_retry_batch_preserves_order`
- `test_async_retry_batch_preserves_order`
- `test_async_retrying`

(2 consecutive clean runs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

entities.name: RunnableRetry._batch()
entities.description: A method used for batch processing in the RunnableRetry component.

entities.name: RunnableRetry._abatch()
entities.description: An asynchronous method used for batch processing in the RunnableRetry component.

entities.name: list.pop(0)
entities.description: A Python list method used to remove and return the first element, which has O(n) complexity.

entities.name: Performance Degradation
entities.description: A performance issue caused by the O(n²) complexity of list.pop(0) in the reassembly loop for large batch sizes.

entities.name: CPython
entities.description: The default implementation of the Python programming language.

entities.name: collections.deque
entities.description: A Python data structure that supports O(1) amortized appends and pops from both ends.

entities.name: .popleft()
entities.description: A method of collections.deque to remove and return an element from the left end, with O(1) amortized complexity.

entities.name: Result Reassembly
entities.description: The process of merging successful inputs with remaining results to form the final batch output.

entities.name: Copilot
entities.description: An AI assistant that co-authored the solution.

extracted_data.summary: The issue addresses a performance problem in `RunnableRetry._batch()` and `_abatch()` where `list.pop(0)` causes O(n²) complexity during result reassembly, proposing a solution to use `collections.deque` and its O(1) `popleft()` method.
